ddls:
CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Full name of the service */,
  "service_short_name" TEXT /* Short name or abbreviation for the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description and dependencies of the service */,
  "default_startup_type" TEXT /* Default startup type for the service */,
  "security_context" TEXT /* Security context under which the service runs */,
  "dependencies" TEXT /* List of dependencies for the service */,
  "required_or_optional" TEXT /* Indication of whether the service is required or optional */,
  "version" TEXT /* Current version of the service */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the service */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Categorization of the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_default_startup_type FOREIGN KEY ("default_startup_type") REFERENCES service_startups ("startup_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_security_context FOREIGN KEY ("security_context") REFERENCES security_contexts ("context_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency relationship */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service that has dependencies */,
  "dependent_service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service that is depended upon */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., Required, Optional) */,
  "dependency_description" TEXT /* Description of the dependency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_dependencies_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_dependencies_dependent_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_startups" (
  "startup_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each startup type */,
  "startup_type" TEXT /* Type of startup (e.g., Automatic, Manual) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the startup type */,
  "startup_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the startup type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("startup_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_contexts" (
  "context_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security context */,
  "context_name" TEXT /* Name of the security context */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the security context */,
  "context_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the security context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("context_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the service (e.g., Running, Stopped) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last status update */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the current status */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last status check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_statuses_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service that was updated */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the update was applied */,
  "update_version" TEXT /* Version number of the update */,
  "update_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_updates_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_compliance" (
  "compliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance check */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service being checked */,
  "compliance_check" TEXT /* Type of compliance check performed */,
  "compliance_date" TEXT /* Date when the compliance check was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the compliance check */,
  "compliance_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the compliance check */,
  "compliance_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the compliance check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compliance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_compliance_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who has access */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access granted (e.g., Read, Write, Full Control) */,
  "access_granted_date" TEXT /* Date when access was granted */,
  "access_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the access level */,
  "access_expiration" TEXT /* Date when the access will expire */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_access_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the organization */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service for which the alert was generated */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was resolved */,
  "alert_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the alert (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., Critical, Warning, Info) */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., System, User) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_alerts_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)
